name: low-power-dashboard-render-mode
description: Create a performance-optimized viewer for embedding in a dashboard. Tests the skill's performance tips section — specifically requestRenderMode, scene3DOnly, widget reduction, and the critical detail that requestRender() must be called after programmatic changes.
prompt: I'm embedding a CesiumJS globe in a monitoring dashboard that shows mostly static data. It'll be running on a tablet with limited battery. Optimize the viewer for minimum power consumption — the globe will only update when new data arrives, not continuously. I want terrain but no unnecessary widgets. Also add a single point entity for our headquarters at coordinates 37.7749 N, 122.4194 W to verify the setup works.
expected_behaviors: Sets maximumRenderTimeChange: Infinity (only render on explicit request)
Enables scene3DOnly: true (since 2D/CV not needed, saves GPU memory)
Disables unnecessary widgets (at minimum: animation, timeline)
Adds a point entity at the specified coordinates
Calls viewer.scene.requestRender() after adding the entity OR uses the entity API which auto-triggers renders
May reduce msaaSamples for additional power savings
visual_expectations: A 3D globe with terrain and a visible point/marker entity near San Francisco (37.77N, 122.42W). Minimal UI chrome. The scene should be static (not continuously animating).

programmatic_checks.description: Locked to 3D mode for GPU savings
Latitude for SF headquarters is correct

screenshots.description: Globe with terrain and a visible point entity near San Francisco

notes: This eval tests the intersection of the viewer-setup skill with the entity skill. The viewer-setup skill should provide enough context for the LLM to correctly set up requestRenderMode, but entity creation technically belongs to cesiumjs-entities. A good viewer-setup skill helps the LLM get the rendering mode right even when combining domains. Common failure: enabling requestRenderMode but not understanding that entities auto-trigger renders (so requestRender() is optional with the Entity API).
